description_long: This object is a compact solar energy infrastructure unit featuring a rectangular base with a prominent, elevated solar panel array mounted on top. The solar panel is composed of multiple dark photovoltaic cells arranged in a grid, supported by a metallic frame. The base is primarily yellow with black and gray accents, and it appears to house electrical components, possibly including an inverter and battery storage. The sides of the base display various access panels, vents, and possibly control interfaces, suggesting maintenance access and system monitoring. The overall design is robust, with the solar panel tilted at an angle to optimize sunlight capture. This infrastructure is suitable for installation in urban environments such as sidewalks, parks, or public plazas, providing renewable energy for local amenities.
description: A compact, yellow solar energy unit with a tilted solar panel array on top and a robust, access-panel-equipped base. Designed for urban settings, it features a metallic frame and visible electrical housing.
description_view_0: Front view showing the wide yellow base, central access panels, and the full width of the tilted solar panel array.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the base, the angled solar panel, and detailed access panels on the end.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the yellow base, additional access panels, and the rear structure of the solar panel frame.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the other end of the base, with visible vents and the side profile of the solar panel.
attribute_panel_orientation: tilted at approximately 30 degrees, orientation appears fixed, azimuth direction not clearly discernible
attribute_mounting_structure_type: ground-mounted with a metallic (likely aluminum) frame and reinforced base
attribute_array_configuration: single row of solar panels, approximately 1x4 configuration
